Clio Coder v0.2.2 is the largest harness revision since the v0.2.0 community
alpha. It retires the CLI-subprocess runtime era in favor of direct
HTTP/native/pi-ai-backed targets with the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) as the
interop surface, and it rebuilds the local-inference hot path around
prompt-prefix stability.

## Highlights

- **Prompt-prefix stability for local models.** One compiled system prompt
  and one deterministic tool surface per session, bounded tool results,
  single-threshold compaction, and per-call timing and cache telemetry in the
  session ledger. On a single-slot llama.cpp backend, the measured first-turn
  gap dropped from roughly a minute to about a second once the prefix is
  resident.
- **ACP interop.** `clio acp` serves ACP v1 over stdio for ACP frontends, and
  dispatch can delegate tasks to external ACP agents while Clio mediates
  permissions and records session and usage metadata in receipts.
- **Concurrent sessions on one machine.** Live routing is session-owned, the
  session ledger appends instead of rewriting, and the shared settings file
  is written through field-level patches under an advisory lock.
- **Curated skills catalog.** A maintainer-reviewed catalog under `skills/`
  with provenance frontmatter and explicit installation; nothing auto-loads.
  First wave: `context-prime`, `context-handoff`, `clio-dev`, `clio-test`.
- **Context engine.** Context window resolution, per-model probes, unified
  token accounting, per-turn snapshots, a `/context-view` overlay, and
  overflow recovery.
- **Reliable local install/uninstall.** `npm run install:local` links a
  deterministic `clio` symlink; `npm run uninstall:local` removes it plus
  selected state, with dry-run and settings/auth preservation options.
- **CLIO.md as a project rulebook.** Custom H2 sections survive parsing and
  reach the model, and this repository ships its own CLIO.md as the
  reference example of the bootstrap format.

## Breaking changes

- The `claude-code-sdk`, `claude-code-cli`, `gemini-cli`, `copilot-cli`,
  `codex-cli`, and `opencode-cli` runtime descriptors are removed. There are
  no built-in subprocess runtimes; external coding agents integrate through
  ACP delegation.
- The `--auto-approve` CLI flag, the `auto_approve` dispatch argument, and
  the Claude-Code-specific supervised dispatch path are removed.
- The legacy advice/default/super operating modes are replaced by a single
  unified operating posture.
- The five-stage compaction ladder is replaced by a single-threshold
  `compaction` settings block. Existing settings files migrate automatically
  on first run.

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/iowarp/clio-coder.git
cd clio-coder
git checkout v0.2.2
npm run install:local
hash -r
clio --version
```

The package is not published to npm for this release; a source checkout is
the supported install path.

## Verification

The deterministic release gate (`npm run ci:release`) passed at tag time:
typecheck, Biome checks, production build, 286 contract/smoke/boundary
tests, and `check-dist` packaging verification. Live model validation is
manual and opt-in through `npm run test:live`.

Full details: [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/iowarp/clio-coder/blob/v0.2.2/CHANGELOG.md)
